title: Nonea monticola
intro: Nonea monticola, also known as Mountain Nonea, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Mountain Nonea include iridoids, phenylethanoid glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Mountain Nonea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Nonea monticola is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The classification of Nonea monticola includes the kingdom Plantae, and the order Boraginales. Within the family Boraginaceae, it belongs to the genus Nonea. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary history and relationships of Nonea monticola.
